mission.title: Install and Configure Tokenetes on Kubernetes
mission.description: Tokenetes is an open source Transaction Tokens (TraTs) Service — an implementation of the IETF OAuth Transaction Tokens draft specification. It enables secure propagation of identity and context across microservices via transaction tokens. Tokenetes uses SPIRE for workload identity and requires tconfigd for managing TraT configurations. A complete installation involves three phases: tconfigd, the Tokenetes service, and Tokenetes agents (sidecars for TraT verification).

steps.title: Install SPIRE (prerequisite)
steps.description:
Tokenetes requires SPIRE for identity attestation. Install SPIRE first:
```bash
helm repo add spiffe https://spiffe.github.io/helm-charts-hardened/
helm repo update
kubectl create namespace spire
helm install spire spiffe/spire --namespace spire
```

steps.title: Install Tokenetes
steps.description:
Deploy Tokenetes from the official manifests:
```bash
kubectl create namespace tokenetes
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/otterize/tokenetes/main/deploy/tokenetes.yaml -n tokenetes
```

steps.title: Configure token exchange
steps.description:
Create a TRAT (Token Request Authorization Template):
```bash
kubectl apply -f - << 'EOF'
apiVersion: tokenetes.otterize.com/v1alpha1
kind: TRAT
metadata:
  name: example-trat
  namespace: tokenetes
spec:
  serviceAccountName: my-service
  audiences:
    - my-api
EOF
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n tokenetes
kubectl get trat -n tokenetes
```

resolution.summary: Tokenetes is installed for SPIFFE-based token exchange and workload identity on Kubernetes.

uninstall.title: Remove Tokenetes
uninstall.description:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace tokenetes
helm uninstall spire -n spire
kubectl delete namespace spire
```

troubleshooting.title: SPIRE agent not connecting
troubleshooting.description:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n spire
kubectl logs -l app=spire-agent -n spire
```

troubleshooting.title: Token exchange failing
troubleshooting.description:
Check Tokenetes logs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app=tokenetes -n tokenetes
```

troubleshooting.title: TRAT not taking effect
troubleshooting.description:
Verify the CRD exists:
```bash
kubectl get crd | grep tokenetes
```

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher with SPIRE deployed for workload identity. The SPIRE server must be configured with the k8sbundle notifier plugin and have ClusterRole permissions for webhook configurations. kubectl and git must be installed on your local machine.
